frames.narrationSummary: Seed the frontier queue with start node A.
Mark A visited immediately so BFS never enqueues it again.
The frontier is not empty, so BFS can expand A next.
Dequeue A; it is now the node BFS expands.
A is not the target, so inspect its neighbors.
Neighbor B is unseen and should join the frontier.
Add B to the visited set before enqueuing it.
Enqueue B at the back of the frontier queue.
Neighbor C is unseen and should join the frontier.
Add C to the visited set before enqueuing it.
Enqueue C at the back of the frontier queue.
The frontier is not empty, so BFS can expand B next.
Dequeue B; it is now the node BFS expands.
B is not the target, so inspect its neighbors.
Neighbor A was already visited, so BFS skips it.
Neighbor D is unseen and should join the frontier.
Add D to the visited set before enqueuing it.
Enqueue D at the back of the frontier queue.
The frontier is not empty, so BFS can expand C next.
Dequeue C; it is now the node BFS expands.
C is not the target, so inspect its neighbors.
Neighbor A was already visited, so BFS skips it.
Neighbor D was already visited, so BFS skips it.
Neighbor E is unseen and should join the frontier.
Add E to the visited set before enqueuing it.
Enqueue E at the back of the frontier queue.
The frontier is not empty, so BFS can expand D next.
Dequeue D; it is now the node BFS expands.
D is not the target, so inspect its neighbors.
Neighbor B was already visited, so BFS skips it.
Neighbor C was already visited, so BFS skips it.
Neighbor F is unseen and should join the frontier.
Add F to the visited set before enqueuing it.

values.visited: A, B, C, D, E, F

frames.narrationSummary: Enqueue F at the back of the frontier queue.
